home.title: Free Student Housing Platform — Find & List Rooms
home.description: OpenHospi is the free, open-source student housing platform for the Netherlands. Verified students only via SURFconext. No fees, no middlemen.

about.title: About OpenHospi — Our Mission & Story
about.description: Learn about OpenHospi's mission to make student housing in the Netherlands fair, free, and accessible for every student.

costs.title: Costs & Transparency — How We Stay Free
costs.description: OpenHospi is free for all students. See exactly what it costs to run the platform and where donations go.

rooms.title: Student Rooms in the Netherlands
rooms.description: Browse available student rooms across Dutch cities. Free, verified, and fair for every student.

costs.question: Is OpenHospi really free?
costs.answer: Yes, OpenHospi is completely free for all students. There are no hidden fees, no premium plans, and no ads. We are funded by donations and sponsorships.

costs.question: How is OpenHospi funded?
costs.answer: OpenHospi is funded through individual donations, university sponsorships, and volunteer open-source contributions. All finances are transparent via Open Collective.

costs.question: Will OpenHospi ever become paid?
costs.answer: No. OpenHospi will never be freemium or pay-to-win. No student will ever have to pay to use the platform or get more visibility.

safety.question: How are users verified on OpenHospi?
safety.answer: Every user logs in through SURFconext, which is managed by their own university or hogeschool. This means only real students of Dutch educational institutions can use the platform.

safety.question: Are my messages private?
safety.answer: Yes. All chat messages on OpenHospi are end-to-end encrypted. Not even OpenHospi can read your conversations.

safety.question: What happens when I report a user?
safety.answer: Reports are reviewed by our team. We take action quickly, including blocking users who violate our terms of service.

findARoom.question: How do I find a room on OpenHospi?
findARoom.answer: Log in with your SURFconext account, complete your profile, browse available rooms by city and price, and send a personal message to houses you like.

findARoom.question: What is hospiteren?
findARoom.answer: Hospiteren is the traditional Dutch way of finding housemates. Houses invite candidates for a visit so both sides can see if it's a good fit before moving in.

features.title: SURFconext verification
features.description: Every user is verified by their own university or hogeschool through SURFconext. Your institution stands behind your profile. No fake profiles.

features.title: End-to-end encrypted chat
features.description: Messages are encrypted between sender and receiver. We cannot read your conversations.

features.title: No trackers
features.description: We don't use third-party trackers, analytics cookies, or sell your data.

features.title: GDPR compliant
features.description: We follow EU data protection regulations. You can export or delete your data anytime.

features.title: Open source
features.description: Our entire codebase is public. Anyone can audit how we handle your data.

features.title: Report & block
features.description: Report inappropriate behavior or block users with one click. We act on reports quickly. You can also reach us at abuse@openhospi.nl.

mission.title: Our mission
mission.description: OpenHospi exists to make student housing in the Netherlands fair, free, and accessible. We believe finding a room shouldn't cost money, and the process should be transparent. OpenHospi will never be freemium or pay-to-win: no one can pay to get more visibility. Every student gets the same chances.

crisis.title: The problem
crisis.description: Students search for rooms through Facebook groups, Instagram, and paid platforms. On commercial sites, you pay to see listings or get priority. That's unfair — the system works for platforms, not for students.

builtBy.title: How it started
builtBy.description: Ruben saw fellow students struggling with Facebook groups, Instagram, and paid platforms. He didn't experience it himself, but saw many around him dealing with it. That's how OpenHospi was born.

openSource.title: Open source & community
openSource.description: OpenHospi is a community project. Anyone can contribute code, translations, or ideas. We're transparent about everything we do.

sections.title: 1. Controller
sections.content: OpenHospi is the controller within the meaning of Art. 4(7) GDPR for the processing of your personal data. OpenHospi is a free, open-source student housing platform for the Netherlands. For privacy questions, contact us at privacy@openhospi.nl. OpenHospi has not designated a data protection officer, as this is not required under Art. 37 GDPR given the nature and scale of our processing activities.

sections.title: 2. Privacy contact
sections.content: For all privacy and data protection inquiries, contact privacy@openhospi.nl. We handle your request free of charge and respond within one month of receipt, in accordance with Art. 12(3) GDPR. For complex or numerous requests, we may extend this period by a further two months. We will inform you of any such extension within one month of receipt of the request, together with the reasons for the delay.

sections.title: 3. Data we collect
sections.content: We collect the following categories of personal data, in accordance with the principle of data minimisation (Art. 5(1)(c) GDPR):
items: Profile data — name, email address, date of birth, gender, study programme, educational institution, bio, lifestyle tags, preferred city, available from date, maximum rent, photos
Housing data — room listings with addresses, coordinates, rent price, room details and photos
Communication data — end-to-end encrypted chat messages (content is unreadable by OpenHospi)
Application data — personal message and application status
Session data — IP address and user agent for security purposes
Push notification tokens — for delivery of notifications to your device
Consent records — your cookie and privacy preferences, timestamps and privacy policy version

We do not collect special categories of personal data as referred to in Art. 9(1) GDPR (including data revealing racial or ethnic origin, political opinions, religious beliefs, health or sexual orientation). If you voluntarily share such data in your profile or messages, we process it on the basis of Art. 9(2)(e) GDPR (manifestly made public by the data subject).

sections.title: 4. SURFconext data
sections.content: When you log in via SURFconext, we receive your name, email address, and educational institution domain. SURFconext is operated by SURF, the Dutch collaborative organisation for IT in education. SURF is an independent controller for the authentication process. We use the data received to verify your student identity and create your account.

sections.title: 5. How we use your data
sections.content: We use your data solely to provide the OpenHospi platform, in accordance with the purpose limitation principle (Art. 5(1)(b) GDPR): matching students with rooms, facilitating communication between seekers and hosts, and managing the housing application process. We do not sell your data, use it for advertising, profile you for marketing purposes, or share it with third parties except as described in this policy.

sections.title: 6. Obligation to provide personal data
sections.content: Profile data (name, email address, study information) is contractually required to use the platform (Art. 13(2)(e) GDPR). Without this data, no account can be created and the platform cannot function. The provision of this data is a contractual requirement, not a statutory obligation. You are free not to create an account if you do not wish to provide this data.

sections.title: 7. Legal basis
sections.content: We process your data on the following legal grounds (Art. 6 GDPR). See our Legal Basis Overview page for a complete breakdown per processing activity.
items: Performance of a contract (Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR) — profile, housing, applications, chat; necessary for the performance of the contract
Consent (Art. 6(1)(a) GDPR) — functional cookies, push notifications, analytics

Legitimate interests (Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR) — session security (IP logging), platform safety (moderation reports). We have balanced these interests against your rights and freedoms and determined that the processing is necessary and proportionate
Legal obligation (Art. 6(1)(c) GDPR) — consent records (demonstrating valid consent in accordance with Art. 7 GDPR)
You may withdraw your consent at any time via Settings > Privacy. The withdrawal of consent shall not affect the lawfulness of processing based on consent before its withdrawal (Art. 7(3) GDPR).

sections.title: 8. Data sharing & processors
sections.content: We share your data only with the following parties, all located within the EU/EEA. All processors operate under data processing agreements pursuant to Art. 28 GDPR. See our Data Processors page for details.
items: Supabase (Dublin, Ireland) — database and file storage (processor)
Vercel (Dublin, Ireland) — web hosting and edge functions (processor)
Upstash (Ireland) — rate limiting via Redis (processor)
SURFconext (Netherlands) — authentication (independent controller, not a processor within the meaning of Art. 28 GDPR)

sections.title: 9. International transfers
sections.content: All your data is stored and processed within the European Economic Area (EEA). We do not transfer personal data to third countries or international organisations. The provisions on transfers (Art. 44 to 49 GDPR) therefore do not apply. All our infrastructure providers are based in Ireland or the Netherlands.

sections.title: 10. Data retention
sections.content: We do not retain your data longer than necessary for the purposes for which it was collected, in accordance with the storage limitation principle (Art. 5(1)(e) GDPR):
items: Profile data, housing listings, and chat messages — retained as long as your account is active
Session IP addresses — anonymised after 30 days
Expired sessions — deleted after 90 days
Report message text — removed 90 days after resolution
Read notifications — deleted after 180 days
Consent record IP addresses — anonymised after 365 days
Account deletion — all data permanently removed via cascading deletes

sections.title: 11. Your rights
sections.content: Under GDPR, you have the following rights with regard to your personal data. You can exercise these rights via Settings > Privacy, or by contacting privacy@openhospi.nl.
items: Right of access (Art. 15 GDPR) — you have the right to obtain a copy of your personal data, via Settings > Privacy > Data export
Right to rectification (Art. 16 GDPR) — you can have inaccurate or incomplete data corrected, via your profile
Right to erasure (Art. 17 GDPR) — you can request erasure of your data, via Settings > Delete account
Right to restriction of processing (Art. 18 GDPR) — you can request restriction of processing while retaining your data

Notification obligation (Art. 19 GDPR) — we notify each recipient to whom personal data have been disclosed of any rectification, erasure, or restriction, unless this proves impossible or involves disproportionate effort
Right to data portability (Art. 20 GDPR) — you can receive your data in a structured, commonly used and machine-readable format (JSON or CSV), via Settings > Privacy > Data export
Right to object (Art. 21 GDPR) — you can object to processing based on legitimate interests

sections.title: 12. Right to lodge a complaint
sections.content: If you believe your data protection rights have been violated, you have the right to lodge a complaint with the Dutch Data Protection Authority (Autoriteit Persoonsgegevens) pursuant to Art. 77 GDPR. You may also lodge a complaint with the supervisory authority in your EU member state of habitual residence.
items: Autoriteit Persoonsgegevens — Postbus 93374, 2509 AJ Den Haag
Telephone — 070 888 85 00
Website — autoriteitpersoonsgegevens.nl

sections.title: 13. Cookies & tracking
sections.content: We use a minimal set of cookies. Essential cookies (session authentication) are strictly necessary for the platform to function and do not require consent. Functional cookies (language preference, sidebar state) require your consent under Dutch telecommunications law (Telecommunicatiewet Art. 11.7a). We do not use tracking cookies or third-party advertising cookies. See our Cookie Policy for full details.

sections.title: 14. End-to-end encryption
sections.content: All chat messages between students and room hosts are end-to-end encrypted (E2EE) using AES-256-GCM with ECDH key exchange, as an appropriate technical measure within the meaning of Art. 32 GDPR and in implementation of data protection by design (Art. 25 GDPR). This means OpenHospi cannot read the content of your private messages. Encryption keys are stored locally on your device and optionally backed up (encrypted) on our servers.

sections.title: 15. Personal data breaches
sections.content: In the event of a personal data breach, OpenHospi will notify the Autoriteit Persoonsgegevens without undue delay and, where feasible, not later than 72 hours after having become aware of it, unless the breach is unlikely to result in a risk to your rights and freedoms (Art. 33 GDPR). Where the breach is likely to result in a high risk to your rights and freedoms, we will communicate the breach to you without undue delay and in clear and plain language (Art. 34 GDPR). The end-to-end encryption of chat messages limits the impact of any breach on communication data, as the encryption renders the data unintelligible to unauthorised persons (Art. 34(3)(a) GDPR).

sections.title: 16. Children's data
sections.content: OpenHospi is exclusively accessible via SURFconext, which requires enrollment at an accredited Dutch educational institution. This means all users are students aged 16 or older, in accordance with Art. 5 UAVG (implementing Art. 8(1) GDPR, which allows member states to set the age threshold at a minimum of 16). We do not knowingly collect personal data from children under 16.

sections.title: 17. Automated individual decision-making
sections.content: OpenHospi does not use automated individual decision-making, including profiling, as referred to in Art. 22 GDPR. Room matching is entirely manual — users browse listings and submit applications themselves. House members vote on applicants through a manual voting process. No algorithms determine who sees which rooms or who gets accepted.

sections.title: 18. Changes to this policy
sections.content: We may update this privacy policy from time to time. Significant changes will be communicated via an in-app notification. The 'last updated' date at the top reflects the most recent revision.

sections.title: What are cookies?
sections.content: Cookies are small text files stored on your device when you visit a website. They help the site remember your preferences and improve your experience. Under Dutch law (Telecommunicatiewet Art. 11.7a), we need your consent for non-essential cookies and similar technologies (such as localStorage).

sections.title: Essential cookies
sections.content: These cookies are strictly necessary for the platform to function. No consent is required under Telecommunicatiewet Art. 11.7a, as they are essential for providing the service you have requested.
items: better-auth.session_token — authenticates your login session (session cookie, deleted when you close your browser)

sections.title: Functional cookies and local storage
sections.content: The following cookies and local storage technologies improve your experience but are not strictly necessary. We request your consent for these in accordance with Telecommunicatiewet Art. 11.7a.
items: sidebar_state — remembers whether the sidebar is open or closed (persistent, localStorage — not a cookie, but local storage in your browser)
NEXT_LOCALE — remembers your language preference (cookie, valid for 1 year)

sections.title: Analytics
sections.content: We use Vercel Analytics, which is cookieless and does not process personally identifiable information. While no consent is technically required, we list it in your consent preferences for full transparency. You can opt out at any time via Settings > Privacy.

sections.title: Managing your preferences
sections.content: You can change your cookie preferences at any time via Settings > Privacy in your account, or by clicking 'Manage preferences' on the cookie banner. For questions about our cookie policy, contact privacy@openhospi.nl.

sections.title: Eligibility
sections.content: OpenHospi is available to students of Dutch educational institutions that are connected to SURFconext.

sections.title: Account responsibilities
sections.content: You are responsible for maintaining the security of your account. Do not share your login credentials or attempt to impersonate others.

sections.title: Acceptable use
sections.content: You agree to use OpenHospi only for finding or offering student housing. Harassment, discrimination, fraud, and spam are prohibited.

sections.title: Content ownership
sections.content: You retain ownership of content you post (profile text, photos, messages). By posting, you grant OpenHospi a license to display this content on the platform.

sections.title: Liability
sections.content: OpenHospi is provided as-is. We are not responsible for the accuracy of listings, the behavior of other users, or any transactions made outside the platform.

sections.title: Governing law
sections.content: These terms are governed by Dutch law. Any disputes will be resolved in the courts of the Netherlands.

sections.title: Contact
sections.content: If you have questions about these terms, contact us at support@openhospi.nl.

processingRegister.title: Processing Register (Art. 30 GDPR)
processingRegister.intro: In accordance with Art. 30(1) GDPR, OpenHospi as controller maintains this register of all personal data processing activities.

0.title: 1. User profiles
items: Purpose — Providing the student housing platform service
Categories of data subjects — Registered students
Data categories — name, email address, date of birth, gender, study programme, educational institution, bio, lifestyle tags, preferred city, available from date, maximum rent
Legal basis — Performance of a contract (Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR)
Recipients — Other platform users (limited profile view)
Transfers outside EEA — No, all processing within the EU/EEA
Retention — Until account deletion
Security — Row-Level Security (RLS), encrypted transport (TLS), role-based access control

1.title: 2. Profile photos
items: Purpose — Profile identification and visual recognition
Categories of data subjects — Registered students
Data categories — up to 5 photos per user
Legal basis — Performance of a contract (Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR)
Recipients — Other platform users (authenticated)
Transfers outside EEA — No, all processing within the EU/EEA
Retention — Until deletion by user or account deletion
Security — Supabase Storage with access policies, encrypted storage

2.title: 3. Room listings
items: Purpose — Housing marketplace functionality and geographic search
Categories of data subjects — Registered students (room hosts)
Data categories — address, city, coordinates, rent price, room details, photos, availability
Legal basis — Performance of a contract (Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR)
Recipients — All authenticated users
Transfers outside EEA — No, all processing within the EU/EEA
Retention — Until deletion by owner or account deletion
Security — RLS policies (owner-only editing), encrypted transport

3.title: 4. Applications
items: Purpose — Matching seekers with rooms and managing the hospiteren process
Categories of data subjects — Registered students (room seekers)
Data categories — personal message, application status
Legal basis — Performance of a contract (Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR)
Recipients — Room owner and house members of the relevant house
Transfers outside EEA — No, all processing within the EU/EEA
Retention — Until withdrawal by seeker or account deletion
Security — RLS policies restricting access to involved parties

4.title: 5. Chat messages
items: Purpose — Communication between seekers and house members
Categories of data subjects — Registered students (conversation participants)
Data categories — end-to-end encrypted message content, timestamps, encrypted session keys
Legal basis — Performance of a contract (Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR)
Recipients — Conversation participants only (content unreadable by OpenHospi)
Transfers outside EEA — No, all processing within the EU/EEA
Retention — Until account deletion
Security — End-to-end encryption (AES-256-GCM with ECDH key exchange), only participants can decrypt

5.title: 6. Session data
items: Purpose — Authentication and security monitoring
Categories of data subjects — Registered students
Data categories — IP address, user agent, session token
Legal basis — Legitimate interests (Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR)
Recipients — None (internal use only)
Transfers outside EEA — No, all processing within the EU/EEA
Retention — IP address anonymised 30 days after session expiry; session record deleted after 90 days
Security — Server-side processing only, encrypted storage

6.title: 7. Moderation data
items: Purpose — Platform safety and abuse prevention
Categories of data subjects — Registered students (reporters and reported users)
Data categories — reports, blocks, temporarily decrypted message text (provided by reporter)
Legal basis — Legitimate interests (Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR)
Recipients — Admin team only
Transfers outside EEA — No, all processing within the EU/EEA
Retention — Decrypted message text automatically deleted 90 days after resolution
Security — Admin-only access, full audit logging

7.title: 8. Consent records
items: Purpose — Demonstrating valid consent (Art. 7 GDPR)
Categories of data subjects — Registered students and website visitors
Data categories — consent purpose, granted/revoked status, IP address, user agent, privacy policy version, timestamp
Legal basis — Legal obligation (Art. 6(1)(c) GDPR)
Recipients — None (internal audit purposes only)
Transfers outside EEA — No, all processing within the EU/EEA
Retention — IP address anonymised after 365 days; consent records retained as long as necessary for evidentiary purposes
Security — Immutable audit trail, encrypted storage

8.title: 9. Push notification subscriptions
items: Purpose — Delivering push notifications to users
Categories of data subjects — Registered students who have enabled push notifications
Data categories — endpoint URL, encryption keys for push service
Legal basis — Consent (Art. 6(1)(a) GDPR)
Recipients — Browser push service (technically necessary for delivery)
Transfers outside EEA — No, all processing within the EU/EEA
Retention — Until user unsubscribes or account deletion
Security — Web Push protocol with end-to-end encryption

dpia.title: Data Protection Impact Assessment (Art. 35 GDPR)
dpia.intro: This Data Protection Impact Assessment evaluates the risks associated with personal data processing on the OpenHospi platform, in accordance with Art. 35(7) GDPR.

0.title: 1. Description of processing
0.content: OpenHospi is a free, open-source student housing platform for the Netherlands. Users authenticate via SURFconext (institutional SSO), create profiles, list rooms, browse listings, apply for rooms, and communicate through end-to-end encrypted chat. All users are verified students at Dutch educational institutions. The processing comprises profile data, housing data, communication data, application data, session data, push notification tokens, and consent records.

1.title: 2. Necessity and proportionality
1.content: Data collection is limited to what is strictly necessary for the platform to function, in accordance with the data minimisation principle (Art. 5(1)(c) GDPR).
items: Profile data enables matching (legal basis: performance of a contract, Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR)
Location data enables geographic search
E2E encrypted chat ensures communication privacy (Art. 25 and Art. 32 GDPR)
No data is sold or shared with third parties for marketing
No automated individual decision-making or profiling is used (Art. 22 GDPR)

2.title: 3. Risk: Profile data processing
items: Risk — Unauthorised access to personal profiles
Likelihood — Low
Impact — Medium
Mitigations — Row-Level Security (RLS) on all database tables. Authentication required via SURFconext. Profile visibility limited to authenticated users
Residual risk — Low

3.title: 4. Risk: Location data (room coordinates)
items: Risk — Precise location tracking via room coordinates
Likelihood — Low
Impact — Medium
Mitigations — Coordinates used only for map display. No location tracking of users. Only visible to authenticated users
Residual risk — Low

4.title: 5. Risk: Chat message content
items: Risk — Interception or unauthorised reading of messages
Likelihood — Very low
Impact — High
Mitigations — End-to-end encryption with AES-256-GCM. Per-message keys wrapped for each recipient. Private keys stored in IndexedDB (client-side). Server never has access to plaintext
Residual risk — Very low

5.title: 6. Risk: Photo storage
items: Risk — Unauthorised access to profile or room photos
Likelihood — Low
Impact — Medium
Mitigations — Supabase Storage with access policies. Photos only accessible to authenticated users. Deletion cascades on account removal
Residual risk — Low

6.title: 7. Risk: SURFconext data
items: Risk — Over-collection from SSO provider
Likelihood — Very low
Impact — Low
Mitigations — Only name, email address, and institution domain are received from SURFconext. No additional attributes requested. SURFconext is managed by SURF (Netherlands)
Residual risk — Very low

7.title: 8. Risk: Moderation — temporarily decrypted messages
items: Risk — Exposure of private message content during moderation
Likelihood — Low (only on user report)
Impact — Medium
Mitigations — Only the reporting user provides the decrypted text. Stored temporarily with 90-day auto-deletion. Admin-only access with full audit logging
Residual risk — Low

8.title: 9. Children (Art. 8 GDPR)
8.content: All users authenticate via SURFconext, which requires enrollment at an accredited Dutch educational institution. This effectively ensures all users are 16 or older, in accordance with Art. 5 UAVG (implementing Art. 8(1) GDPR). No separate age verification is needed.

9.title: 10. Conclusion
9.content: The processing activities of OpenHospi present acceptable residual risks after implementation of mitigations. The platform implements data protection by design and by default (Art. 25 GDPR) through end-to-end encryption, Row-Level Security, data minimisation, automated retention rules, and transparent processing. No high residual risks remain that would require prior consultation with the Autoriteit Persoonsgegevens under Art. 36 GDPR.

dataProcessors.title: Data Processors (Art. 28 GDPR)
dataProcessors.intro: OpenHospi uses the following third-party processors for the processing of personal data. All processors are located within the EU/EEA. No personal data is transferred outside the European Economic Area.

0.title: 1. Supabase (Database & Storage)
items: Role — Processor: database hosting (PostgreSQL) and file storage (profile photos, room photos)
Location — Dublin, Ireland (eu-west-1)
Data processed — All personal data stored in the database, uploaded photos
Data processing agreement — Data processing agreement pursuant to Art. 28 GDPR in place (Supabase Data Processing Agreement)
Security — Encryption at rest and in transit, SOC 2 Type II certified

1.title: 2. Vercel (Web Hosting & Edge)
items: Role — Processor: web application hosting, serverless functions, edge network
Location — Dublin, Ireland (eu-west-1)
Data processed — HTTP requests (IP address, user agent) in server logs
Data processing agreement — Data processing agreement pursuant to Art. 28 GDPR in place (Vercel Data Processing Addendum)
Security — Automatic HTTPS, DDoS protection, SOC 2 Type II certified

2.title: 3. Upstash (Redis — Rate Limiting)
items: Role — Processor: Redis database for rate limiting and abuse prevention
Location — Ireland (AWS eu-west-1)
Data processed — Anonymised rate limit counters (user ID hashes). No personal data stored
Data processing agreement — Data processing agreement pursuant to Art. 28 GDPR in place (Upstash DPA)
Security — Encryption at rest and in transit

3.title: 4. SURFconext (SSO/OIDC Provider)
items: Role — Independent controller for the authentication process (not a processor within the meaning of Art. 28 GDPR)
Location — Netherlands (SURF infrastructure)
Data processed — Authentication tokens, name, email address, institution domain (received during login)

Data processing agreement — SURF is not a processor within the meaning of Art. 28 GDPR, but an independent controller for the authentication process. The collaboration is governed by the SURFconext Terms of Use and Dutch law
Security — SAML/OIDC protocols, institutional-grade security, managed by SURF

legalBasis.title: Legal Basis Overview (Art. 6 GDPR)
legalBasis.intro: This page provides an overview of the legal basis for each data processing activity at OpenHospi, in accordance with Art. 6 GDPR.

0.title: 1. Performance of a contract — Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR
0.content: The following processing activities are necessary for the performance of our contract with you (providing the student housing platform):
items: User profile creation and management
Profile photo uploads
Room listing creation and management
Application submission and processing
Chat messaging (end-to-end encrypted)
House membership management
Voting on applications

1.title: 2. Consent — Art. 6(1)(a) GDPR
1.content: The following processing activities are based on your explicit consent, which you can withdraw at any time via Settings > Privacy. The withdrawal of consent shall not affect the lawfulness of processing based on consent before its withdrawal (Art. 7(3) GDPR).
items: Functional cookies and local storage (sidebar state, language preference)
Push notification delivery
Analytics (Vercel Analytics — cookieless, no PII)

2.title: 3. Legitimate interests — Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR
2.content: The following processing activities are based on our legitimate interest in platform security and user safety. We have balanced these interests against your rights and freedoms and determined that the processing is proportionate and necessary.
items: Session data (IP address, user agent) — for security monitoring
Moderation data (reports, blocks) — for platform safety
Temporarily decrypted message text — for investigating reported messages

3.title: 4. Legal obligation — Art. 6(1)(c) GDPR
3.content: The following processing activities are necessary to comply with legal obligations:
items: Consent records (immutable audit trail) — to demonstrate valid consent in accordance with Art. 7 GDPR
Data request processing — to fulfil data subject rights (Art. 15 to 22 GDPR)
Processing restriction records — to comply with the right to restriction of processing (Art. 18 GDPR)

4.title: 5. No automated individual decision-making
4.content: OpenHospi does not use automated individual decision-making, including profiling, as referred to in Art. 22 GDPR. Room matching is entirely manual — users browse listings and submit applications themselves. House members vote on applicants through a manual voting process. No algorithms determine who gets housing.

5.title: 6. Contact
5.content: If you have questions about the legal basis for processing your data, contact us at privacy@openhospi.nl. You also have the right to lodge a complaint with the Autoriteit Persoonsgegevens (Postbus 93374, 2509 AJ Den Haag, tel. 070 888 85 00, autoriteitpersoonsgegevens.nl).
